biography.value:
Solveig the Burner is a former woodcutter turned charcoal-burner who has lived alone in the Ashen Woods (the remains of the Great Greenweald) for three years. She is thin—terribly thin—with skin stained grey-black by years of charcoal dust. Her clothes are ash-coloured furs. She carries a handaxe and a leather satchel.

Solveig refused to leave when the Great Greenweald was put to the torch. She burns charcoal for fuel, navigates by root-patterns beneath the ash, and avoids blighted beasts. She hasn't spoken to another person in four months.

She is a stubborn, practical survivor—not a quest-giver, not a tragic victim, but a human who decided the Ashen Woods are her home now.
biography.director:
Solveig appears in Beat 6, Part 6 (The Charcoal Cathedral)—a Point of Interest between Montage Rounds 2 and 3.

She shows herself only if the PCs leave an offering at the 'cathedral' altar. Once she trusts them:

  • 'You're heading for the green place.' (Not a question.) She knows about the Green Heart—seen 'a wall of thorns and light' from a distance.
  • Root-system lore: 'The ground is alive under the dead. The roots glow at night—amber, like your friend's eye.' (Connecting to Lew's amber eye.)
  • Rootgnaw warning: 'There are things that chew the green roots. Big as dogs. Bigger.'
  • If treated well: shares a shortcut through the cathedral (+1 edge on one test in Round 3).

She wants news and a knife (hers is worn to a nub). She will talk for hours if allowed.

If harmed or robbed: vanishes into the ash. She knows these woods better than anyone alive. They will not find her.

In Beat 8 (return journey), the offering spot is empty. A charcoal stick broken in half is left—'Either she's moved on, or she's telling us to.'

description.value:
Solveig has lived alone in the Ashen Woods for three years. She navigates by root-patterns beneath the ash, burns charcoal for fuel, and avoids blighted beasts. She knows these woods better than anyone alive.

If harmed or robbed, she vanishes into the ash and cannot be found. She chooses who sees her.
description.director: Solveig only reveals herself to those who leave an offering at the Charcoal Cathedral altar. She is not encountered by chance.

description.value:
Solveig knows the root-patterns beneath the Ashen Woods. She can see where Yggdrasil's roots still glow amber at night, and where they've gone dark. 'The ground is alive under the dead. The roots glow at night—amber, like your friend's eye.'

She knows the way to the Green Heart ('a wall of thorns and light') and can provide a shortcut through the Charcoal Cathedral that grants +1 edge on one test in Montage Round 3.
description.director: The root-glow connecting to Lew's amber eye is an important lore thread. Don't explain it—just let the description echo. Players will draw the connection themselves.

description.value: Solveig warns the PCs about the Rootgnaw Brood: 'There are things that chew the green roots. Big as dogs. Bigger.' This prepares the party for the Rootgnaw encounter in Beat 6's second combat.
description.director: This foreshadows the Rootgnaw Grub and Queen encounter. Let the warning be vague enough to build tension but specific enough to be useful—the PCs know something big is eating the roots.
